name: Ve
description: Ve is one of the three brothers, along with Odin and Vili, who are credited with creating the world in Norse mythology. He is the son of Borr and Bestla. Ve, along with his brothers, slew the giant Ymir, and from Ymir's body, they fashioned the world. Ve is particularly associated with granting humans the gifts of speech and the five senses. While Odin gave humans their spirit and life, and Vili provided intelligence and movement, Ve bestowed upon them the ability to hear, see, and articulate their thoughts and experiences. This contribution is crucial to the creation of humankind, making Ve an integral part of the Norse creation narrative. His role, although less emphasized than Odin's, is essential for the complete formation of humanity and their capacity to interact with the world around them. Ve's contributions highlight the interconnectedness of the three brothers' roles in shaping the cosmos and its inhabitants. He is sometimes associated with the element of sound and the power of communication. He is a figure of creation and vital to the existence of humanity.
subtitle: Giver of Speech and the Senses

keyMyths.title: The Slaying of Ymir
keyMyths.description: Ve, along with his brothers Odin and Vili, slew the giant Ymir, using his body to create the world.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Humans
keyMyths.description: Ve bestowed the gift of speech and the senses upon humans, completing their creation with Odin and Vili.

keyMyths.title: The Division of Creation
keyMyths.description: The three brothers divided the responsibilities of creation, each contributing essential elements to the world and its inhabitants.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
